narrative.headline: What 2015 Ford Escape owners report most
narrative.summary: The dominant pattern in owner complaints centers on powertrain and engine behavior, with owners reporting stalling, hesitation on acceleration, and warning messages prompting dealer visits shortly after purchase. Electrical faults and camera reliability round out the picture, with issues appearing both in safety-critical systems and everyday convenience features. Several complaint areas are associated with crashes or fires, indicating that some reported failures occurred under active driving conditions.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle stalling at traffic lights and hesitating during acceleration, with some experiencing these problems within days of purchase and requiring extended dealer repair visits. · 4 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report dashboard warning messages directing them to a dealer immediately, where the cooling temperature sensor has been cited as the finding after diagnostic evaluation. · 8 fire · 4 crash · 5 injured

issues.sample: STARTED CAR AND THE ALARM WENT OFF ON THE DASH SAYING THE FRONT PASSENGER DOOR WAS AJAR. WE OPENED THE DOOR AND LOOKED AT THE DOOR TO MAKE SURE NO ONE HAD HIT THE CAR. NO DAMAGE COULD BE FOUND. SHUT CAR OFF AND RESTARTED. ALARM CONTINUED WITH DASH READING FRONT PASSENGER DOOR AJA
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report false door-ajar warnings appearing on the dash at startup, with the alert persisting even after inspecting the door and finding no visible damage. · 10 fire · 2 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: Owners report the backup camera cutting out after only a brief moment of operation, particularly in cold temperatures or shaded parking conditions, despite fuses checking out as functional. · 2 crash
